stdout:
**Findings**

- Blocking: Transition-time graph evaluation still touches the whole repository. [crates/jit/src/commands/mod.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/commands/mod.rs:635) calls `self.storage.list_issues()?`, then [line 637](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/commands/mod.rs:637) builds a `DependencyGraph` from every issue before filtering to the neighborhood. The success criterion says transition-time evaluation must touch only the affected issue’s graph neighborhood, not the whole repository. The new comment at [lines 629-631](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/commands/mod.rs:629) explicitly acknowledges the repo-wide read, so this acceptance criterion is not met.

- Blocking: Public/internal docs still contradict the new behavior. [crates/jit/src/validation/rules.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/validation/rules.rs:186) says graph rules “run only in `jit validate` and gate checkers, never on write,” and [crates/jit/src/validation/graph.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/validation/graph.rs:3) repeats “NEVER on the write path.” The transition implementation now runs graph rules during state-transition writes, so the documentation criterion is not fully satisfied even though the concept doc and example `rules.toml` comments were updated.

**Acceptance Criteria**

- Enforcing graph rules block transitions with exit-code-compatible `TransitionBlockedError`: implemented in [crates/jit/src/commands/mod.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/commands/mod.rs:590) and rendered via [crates/jit/src/errors.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/errors.rs:172).
- Non-enforcing failures are returned as warnings: implemented at [crates/jit/src/commands/mod.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/commands/mod.rs:586).
- Neighborhood-only criterion: failed, as noted above.
- Blocked transitions are event logged: implemented at [crates/jit/src/commands/mod.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/commands/mod.rs:603).
- Documentation: partially failed due stale Rust docs noted above.

**Architecture And Separation**

The main orchestration lives in `commands/`, rule evaluation stays in `validation/`, and event types stay in `domain/`. I did not find a direct storage-boundary violation in the new production code.

**Functional Paradigm**

No blocking issue beyond the repository-wide graph construction. The new command code is imperative, but this is command orchestration and not itself a review failure.

**Correctness And Invariants**

Gate/dependency guards still run before graph enforcement on done transitions. State-change events are preserved after successful saves. Blocked graph transitions append a `transition_blocked` event before returning.

**Safety**

No unsafe code found in the reviewed diff.

**Testing**

The issue’s `cargo-ci` gate is recorded as passed with `cargo fmt --all -- --check && cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings && cargo test --workspace`. I also ran `./scripts/jit-validate.sh`; it passed with label warnings only, which the review instructions allow.

**CLI Contract**

The blocking error is extended into JSON details through [crates/jit/src/output.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/output.rs:514). I did not find a new CLI command lacking `--json`.

**Dependencies And Prior Feedback**

The prerequisite issue `cca3e80b` is done. `run_history` is empty, so there is no prior review feedback to re-check.

VERDICT: FAIL
